FULL CONTENT:
# Compose

PRODUCT FEATURES

# Compose

Compose is a web-based music composition and notation tool that enables users to import, edit, create, assign, and share their own music. With Compose, teachers can create original notation from scratch or import previously made notation files. Compose is included with all teacher, student, and free subscriptions.

![Compose feature interface showing music notation being drafted with in-app guidance.](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/61f97639c5531d06236e2e029c7039c067ee1bfb.webp)

## What You Can Do with Compose

![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/save-alt.svg)

### Import

Import your own sheet music from MIDI, MXL, MusicXML, or SMZ files.

![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/edit.svg)

### Edit

Edit the music notation from any file that has been imported.

![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/add-circle.svg)

### Create

Compose original musical works or exercises from anywhere.

![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/assignment-turned-in.svg)

### Assign

Assign content to students, whether it was imported or created within Compose.

![Student playing violin in classroom setting](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/834ea84b71cf6b18a4622c2ef01891a5e754d44f.webp)

## Create and Share

Whether you’re writing new music for your ensembles to practice or crafting exercises to teach music composition or music theory fundamentals to beginner students, Compose makes it easy. You (and your students) can compose music in no time using its simple, intuitive interface.

When sharing content from Compose, it can easily be assigned to individual students or an entire ensemble. Once opened in the Practice tool, students can play the piece along with full MakeMusic Cloud accompaniment and real-time assessment.

![Student composing music on a laptop with notation overlay](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/6773c0a11ef0b12ca7e170e7faecaab3b9cdfab2-1.webp)

## Inspire Creation

Music composition and notation are the most important skills any musician can learn. Writing music helps beginner musicians master fundamentals of theory, arrangement, and collaboration.

Encourage your students to practice writing their own sheet music and watch how quickly their musical understanding grows. Learning basic composition techniques like developing melodies, chord progressions, and instrumentation is a key aspect of any music education program.

VIDEO TUTORIAL

## Get to Know Compose

FAQs

## Frequently Asked Questions

Have questions? We have the pitch perfect answers.

### Is Compose included with my MakeMusic Cloud subscription?

![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Vector-3.svg)
![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Vector-4.svg)
![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Vector-4.svg)

Yes! All MakeMusic Cloud subscriptions include access to Compose.

### Can I use Compose to teach my students how to write music?

![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Vector-3.svg)
![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Vector-4.svg)
![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Vector-4.svg)

Absolutely. Compose is a great tool for students to learn music composition because of its simple, point-and-click interface and intuitive design. Teachers can assign simple music theory exercises, encourage exploration of songwriting, and teach composition techniques all in one app.

### Are there guides that can walk me through the basics of Compose?

![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Vector-3.svg)
![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Vector-4.svg)
![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Vector-4.svg)

There are a number of handy guides that can get you started in Compose. We recommend starting with the [Compose Getting Started guide](https://help.makemusic.com/hc/en-us/sections/15135327076631-Compose), then check out our free [ready-made music composition lessons and activities](https://www.makemusic.com/compose-yourself/).

### Can Compose replace a tool like Finale or Sibelius?

![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Vector-3.svg)
![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Vector-4.svg)
![](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Vector-4.svg)

Compose is an excellent tool for teachers creating exercises and pieces of music for practice, as well as for beginner musicians learning the basics of music composition and notation. It is not meant for composing full symphonic works or writing complex music notation.
Compose’s power is in its simplicity. With a simple point-and-click interface, you or your students will be creating music in no time. Plus, if you have written music or created exercises in another notation program, you can easily export it as MusicXML or MIDI and import it into Compose!

## Stay **up to date**

Discover practical music tips, repertoire, and more delivered directly to you!

![MakeMusic, Inc](https://wpmedia-prod-makemusic-com.s3.us-west-1.amazonaws.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/MakeMusic_Logo_TM_Horizontal_White_NoSpace3.svg)
![Facebook](https://wpmedia-prod-makemusic-com.s3.us-west-1.amazonaws.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/Facebook.svg)
![Youtube](https://wpmedia.makemusic.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/YouTube.svg)

# Top 5 Features to Look for In a Sheet Music Creator in 2025

![Top 5 Features to Look for In a Sheet Music Creator in 2025](https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1500067803284-4304564c8655?crop=entropy&cs=tinysrgb&fit=max&fm=jpg&ixid=MnwxMTc3M3wwfDF8c2VhcmNofDE4OHx8Y29tcHV0ZXIlMjBtdXNpY3xlbnwwfHx8fDE2NzkzODc1NTI&ixlib=rb-4.0.3&q=80&w=2000)

A **sheet music creator** is a tool that allows you to compose, edit, and print sheet music with greater flexibility and control. Whether you’re a composer, arranger, performer, or music teacher, the right software can enhance your creative process. And the best part is that you don't need to be a tech whiz or music theory expert to use it and have fun.

While writing music by hand has its charm, **digital notation software** offers key advantages:  
  
✔️ **Saves time**  
✔️ **Facilitates collaboration**  
✔️ **Protects your work from being lost**

In this guide, we’ll explore **f**ive essential features to look for when choosing a [**music notation software**](https://flat.io/?ref=blog.flat.io) in 2025. Get ready to unleash your inner musician! 🎶

![](https://blog.flat.io/content/images/2025/02/Group-39212-7.png)

## 1. Essential Features in a sheet music creator

## 

A well-designed **sheet music creator** should provide the fundamental tools necessary for accurate composition. These include:

These are the **core functions** of any notation software. Before considering advanced features, make sure your chosen tool covers these basics.

By the way, if you are unfamiliar with any of the above concepts, you can check out Flat’s resources on [music theory](https://blog.flat.io/tag/music-theory/).

## 2. Collaboration and sharing

[Collaborating and sharing](https://flat.io/collaboration?ref=blog.flat.io) your work with other composers is a very popular feature that many musicians and composers use nowadays. Sometimes you’re just looking for feedback from your fellow composers, and sometimes you’ll want to work on music pieces simultaneously with others; you’ll want a sheet music creator that lets you do both.

Modern **sheet music creators** should allow musicians to **collaborate in real time**, making the creative process more dynamic. Whether you’re:  
✅ Seeking **feedback** from fellow composers  
✅ **Co-writing** a piece with musicians across the globe

Collaboration features can **streamline workflows and speed up composition**.

Here at the [Flat Community](https://flat.io/community/popular?ref=blog.flat.io), we always get excited when we see composition collaborations between several composers living in different parts of the world. Having a sharing feature really brings composers together and helps them create sheet music much faster and in a more fun and efficient way.

![](https://blog.flat.io/content/images/icon/icon-256x256-1.png)
![](https://blog.flat.io/content/images/thumbnail/Blog-cover---collaborating-with-other-composers.png)

## 3. Transposition tool

If you’re planning on writing music scores that include different elements or vocal ranges, a [transposition tool](https://help.flat.io/en/music-notation-software/transpose/?ref=blog.flat.io) will be very helpful. Transposing music **manually** is time-consuming and increases the risk of errors. A built-in transposition tool simplifies this process, allowing you to:  
  
🎵 Shift a composition into **different keys** with ease.  
🎵 Adapt music for **various instruments and vocal ranges.**  
🎵 **Explore alternative keys** to inspire new ideas.

A strong transposition feature is particularly useful for performers who need music in a key suited to their instrument or voice. Don't overlook transposition when choosing the right sheet music creator for yourself.

## 4. Custom Keyboard Shortcuts

## 

Efficiency is key when composing. Customizable keyboard shortcuts help you:  
  
⌨️ Speed up note entry and editing  
⌨️ Navigate scores quickly without relying on menus

For example, in [Flat](https://flat.io/?ref=blog.flat.io), you can use *Cmd + A* to select the entire score instead of manually scrolling. Look for a **p**rogram that allows shortcut customization so you can **work faster and more comfortably**.

## 5. Playback, Audio Features and Synchronization

When composing, playback is essential for refining your music. A **good playback system** allows you to:

🎧 Listen to your piece as you compose.  
🎚️ Adjust tempo and timing for greater precision.

Beyond basic playback, a **synchronize audio recording feature** can be incredibly useful. For composers and performers who:

🎵 Want to compare different performances.  
🎵 Transcribe recorded music into notation.

An audio sync tool lets you align the score with an audio track, making it easier to edit, adjust, and analyze performance. This ensures every note is placed correctly and that all instruments are in sync.

When choosing a your music notation tool make sure it offers customizable playback settings and audio synchronization to enhance your workflow.

## Bonus features: User-friendly interface and support

## 

While not strictly musical, a user-friendly interface and strong customer support are essential when choosing your software.

✨ A **clean, intuitive design** saves time and frustration, allowing you to focus on composing rather than figuring out the software. You don’t want a tool that takes months to learn—better to spend that time creating new scores!

✨ **Reliable customer support** ensures quick solutions when issues arise, preventing unnecessary disruptions to your workflow.

At Flat, users appreciate our responsive support team:  
  
💬 *"Your support staff is fantastic and answers quickly!"*  
💬 *"The customer service is excellent compared to other providers."*

These details may seem small, but they make a huge difference in your overall experience.

![](https://blog.flat.io/content/images/2025/02/Group-39213-4.png)

## Conclusion

## 

The best sheet music creator is one that helps you compose professional-quality music efficiently while keeping the process intuitive and enjoyable. Look for software that:

✔️ Provides essential notation tools for precise composition.  
✔️ Includes powerful features like playback, transposition, and collaboration.  
✔️ Enhances workflow efficiency without unnecessary complexity.

🎵 Try Flat today and see if it’s the right fit for your music composition needs!

Best Music Composition Softwares: 2024 Updated Guide
====================================================

*   [Artists](https://nonoki.com/blog/category/artists/)
*    February 20, 2024 

![Image 2: music composition software](https://nonoki.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/studio-1003136_1280-1024x682.jpg)

In today’s world, **music promotion** and **creating your own songs** are more accessible than ever. However, choosing the right **music composition software** can be a challenge, especially for **emerging artists** who don’t have a large budget yet.

In this article, we will present you with an **updated guide** to the **best music composition software**, divided into three categories: professional options, free options, and other alternatives. We will also provide you with some tips on choosing the software that best suits your needs.

**Music Composition****Professional Options:**
----------------------------------------------

*   **FL Studio:**One of the most popular options,with an intuitive interface and a wide range of tools for composition,production,and mixing.[Ideal for beginners and professionals alike.](https://www.softwareadvice.com/audio-editing/fl-studio-profile/reviews/)
*   **Ableton Live:**Famous for its focus on live music creation,it also offers powerful composition tools and a flexible interface.Ideal for electronic music producers and DJs.
*   **Logic Pro:**Apple’s music production software,offers a wide range of tools and features.Ideal for composing,producing,and mixing music.
*   **Cubase:**A software with a long history and a dedicated user base.It offers a wide range of features and tools.Ideal for professional composers.
*   **Pro Tools:**The industry standard for audio recording and mixing,also offers powerful composition tools.Ideal for composers working on large-scale projects.

**Music Composition Free Options:**
-----------------------------------

*   **MuseScore:**A free and open-source music notation software.Ideal for composing scores and for beginners who want to learn the basics of music notation.
*   **LMMS:**A free and open-source music production software that offers many of the same features as paid programs.Ideal for beginners who want to experiment with music composition and production.
*   **Audacity:**A free and open-source audio editing software.Although not specifically designed for music composition,it can be used to create simple music and to edit audio files.
*   **GarageBand:**Apple’s music production software for Mac.It is free for Mac users and offers an intuitive interface and a wide range of tools for beginners.

**Other Options:**
------------------

*   **Sibelius:**A professional music notation software with a wide range of features.Ideal for composers who need to create complex scores.
*   **Finale:**Another professional music notation software with an intuitive interface and a wide range of tools.Ideal for composers who want to create high-quality scores.
*   **Reason:**A music production software that is based on a modular environment.Ideal for composers who want to create experimental electronic music.

**Tips for Choosing the Right Software:**
-----------------------------------------

*   **Consider your budget:**Professional software is usually more expensive than free options.
*   **Define your experience level:**Some software is easier to use than others.
*   **Think about your musical style:**Some software is better suited for certain musical genres than others.
*   **Try different options:**The best way to find the right software for you is to try different options.

**Nonoki: Your Ally for Music Promotion**
-----------------------------------------

At Nonoki, [we want to help you get your music heard by the world](https://nonoki.com/blog/music-platforms-for-uploading-your-songs/). We offer a platform where you can **publish your songs**, create playlists, connect with other artists and fans, and much more.

**What do you need to upload a song to Nonoki?**
------------------------------------------------

*   An audio or video file.
*   A cover art for your song.
*   Information about the song,such as title,artist,genre and year of release.

**How does Nonoki help you promote your music?**
------------------------------------------------

*   **Allows you to distribute your music on major streaming platforms:**Spotify,Apple Music,Deezer,etc.
*   **Offers you tools to create social media promotion campaigns.**
*   **Gives you access to a community of artists and music fans.**

## Music Notation

Even if Pizzicato Composition Pro does not handle page layout and
printing of scores, it provides you several tools to work your music
composition on the basis of standard music notation.

You can export your composition as a MIDI file, an audio (wave) file or
as a musicXML file, for instance to burn a CD or prepare the page layout
and printing of the score with a notation software like Finale, Sibelius
or Notion.

## Music Composition and Arrangement

Composing music consists of finding an idea and structuring this idea
into a full music composition.

Pizzicato gives you a series of tools to find an idea and to develop this
idea. It DOES NOT compose for you! You are the composer.

These tools help you explore musical possibilities with unmatched ease.

At every step of a music composition, you must make choices that will
finally produce a unique composition.

With Pizzicato, you make these choices with various practical tools that
help you to sort out the possibilities by listening.

Your choice is made according to your personal taste and to the
atmosphere or the feelings you want your music to express.

These tools form an intuitive composition environment.

# Best Music Tools For Composers & Musicians In 2025

![Best Music Tools For Composers & Musicians In 2025](//kraftgeek.com/cdn/shop/articles/Best_Music_Tools_577fba94-03e7-4995-9d40-745747553436.jpg?v=1742807042&width=1600)

Kraft Geek |
March 23, 2025

Great ideas can come and go quickly, especially in music. Without the right tools, composers and musicians may struggle to capture melodies, arrange harmonies, or structure their compositions effectively.  

Whether you're a beginner, a teacher, or a professional musician, having reliable tools makes the process smoother and more efficient. The right software or equipment can help bring musical ideas to life, from simple melodies to complex orchestral arrangements. 

## Choosing The Right Music Software

Music software plays a huge role in composition, notation, and arrangement. Some programs are built for beginners, while others cater to professionals handling large-scale projects. Each software comes with unique strengths, making it important to choose one that fits individual needs. 

### **1. MuseScore**

[MuseScore](https://musescore.com/) is a widely used, free music notation software known for its simplicity. It allows musicians to write sheet music, edit scores, and share compositions. It is user-friendly and suitable for beginners and professionals alike.  

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/musescore.jpg?v=1742714047)

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/musescore.jpg?v=1742714047)

The software supports various musical arrangements, and users can export files in multiple formats, including XML, to collaborate with other musicians. MuseScore is ideal for beginners and hobbyists. However, it lacks advanced page formatting, making it less suitable for professionals needing complex layouts. 

### **2. Avid Sibelius**

[Sibelius](https://www.avid.com/sibelius) is a top choice for professionals, students, and educators. It is developed by Avid, the company behind Pro Tools, making it a powerful tool for music composition. It offers multiple versions to cater to different skill levels, from beginners to experts.  

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/Avid_Sibelius.jpg?v=1742714126)

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/Avid_Sibelius.jpg?v=1742714126)

The software includes a wide range of instruments, an extensive sound library, and automation tools that simplify the composition process. It also features automatic transcription capabilities, making it easier for musicians to convert audio into sheet music. 

### **3. LilyPond**

Unlike traditional music notation software, [LilyPond](https://lilypond.org/) uses text-based input to generate high-quality sheet music. This makes it similar to a programming language, which may require a learning curve for new users.  

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/lilypond.jpg?v=1742714409)

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/lilypond.jpg?v=1742714409)

However, once mastered, it allows for high-quality engraving with precise control over notation details. LilyPond is designed for musicians who value aesthetics and advanced typesetting features, making it a great option for producing professional-looking sheet music. 

### **4. Finale**

[Finale](https://www.finalemusic.com/) is one of the most well-known names in music notation. It offers powerful features that allow users to create everything from simple lead sheets to full orchestral scores. It provides various tools for writing music, including chord symbols, tablature, and drum notation.  

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/finale.jpg?v=1742714412)

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/finale.jpg?v=1742714412)

The software supports third-party plugins and add-ons, allowing users to customize their workflow. Finale is widely used in the sheet music publishing industry and musical theater. It is a strong option for professionals who need detailed control over their compositions. 

### **5. Steinberg Dorico**

[Dorico](https://www.steinberg.net/dorico/) is a newer entrant in the music notation software space but has quickly gained recognition for its modern features and intuitive workflow. It features an intuitive interface that automatically adjusts notation as users write, reducing the need for manual corrections. The software includes a MIDI Key Editor, allowing composers to fine-tune performances within the notation environment. 

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/Steinberg_Dorico.jpg?v=1742714424)

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/Steinberg_Dorico.jpg?v=1742714424)

With multiple versions available, including a free option, Dorico caters to musicians at all levels. Dorico is ideal for composers and arrangers looking for an efficient workflow with smart automation. 

### **6. Notion 6**

[Notion 6](https://intl.presonus.com/pages/notion) is gaining popularity due to its interactive features, including a virtual fretboard, keyboard, and drum pad. It supports multiple input methods, including MIDI controllers and handwriting recognition on touch devices.

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/notion_6.jpg?v=1742714421)

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/notion_6.jpg?v=1742714421)

The software provides a unique Sequencer Overlay, which displays both traditional notation and MIDI output, helping musicians refine their compositions. It also includes high-quality playback sounds recorded by professional musicians, making it a great option for composers looking for realistic audio representation of their scores. 

### **7. Noteflight**

[Noteflight](https://www.noteflight.com/) is a web-based music notation tool that allows users to create, edit, and share music directly from their browsers. It is widely used in educational settings due to its compatibility with learning management systems and its collaborative features.  ![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/noteflight.jpg?v=1742714419)

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/noteflight.jpg?v=1742714419)

The platform offers both free and paid plans, providing flexibility for different users. The active online community allows musicians to share compositions, receive feedback, and work on projects together, making it an excellent choice for students and independent songwriters. 

### **8. MagicScore Maestro 8**

[MagicScore](https://www.musicaleditor.com/music-notation-software.html) [Maestro 8](https://www.musicaleditor.com/music-notation-software.html) is an affordable notation software that provides robust tools for composing, editing, and arranging music. It provides a range of templates to help users get started with compositions quickly.  

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/MagicScore_Maestro_8.jpg?v=1742714418)

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/MagicScore_Maestro_8.jpg?v=1742714418)

Despite its slightly outdated interface, it remains a solid option for composers looking for an affordable yet functional notation tool. The software includes various customization options, enabling users to refine their scores for professional presentation. 

### **9. Forte Notation**

[Forte Notation](https://www.fortenotation.com/en/) offers different versions to cater to hobbyists and professionals on a budget. It offers multiple versions with different capabilities, from basic notation features to advanced options such as MIDI recording and XML import. The premium version includes scanning tools that allow musicians to transcribe existing sheet music easily. 

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/Forte_Notation.jpg?v=1742714410)

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/Forte_Notation.jpg?v=1742714410)

Forte is a good choice for those seeking an inexpensive yet functional music notation tool. While it provides useful features, users should compare its functionality with other mid-tier software to determine if it offers the best value. 

### **10. Apple Logic Pro X**

[Apple Logic Pro X](https://www.apple.com/logic-pro/) is a powerful digital audio workstation that includes a built-in score editor for notation. Musicians can record MIDI performances and automatically generate sheet music that can be edited and refined. The software offers extensive customization options for note display, dynamics, and phrasing.  

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/Apple_Logic_Pro_X.jpg?v=1742714414)

![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0652/2277/0926/files/Apple_Logic_Pro_X.jpg?v=1742714414)

It also allows users to create lead sheets, full orchestral scores, and chord grids for various instruments. While it is not primarily a notation software, its integration with Logic’s production tools makes it an excellent choice for composers who also work with digital music production. 

## **Product Recommendations**

## **Conclusion**

Having the right music tools can significantly improve efficiency and creativity in composition and arrangement. Whether you're writing sheet music for personal use, teaching, or publishing professional scores, there is software designed to meet every need. By exploring different options, musicians can find the tools that best suit their workflow and artistic vision.

enriched_points.point_title: Importance of Intuitive Design for Accessibility
enriched_points.expanded_content: An intuitive design ensures users of all skill levels, including beginners, can easily navigate music composition tools, fostering creativity without technical barriers.
Interactive features like real-time collaboration and customizable templates increase user engagement, enabling composers to actively participate in creative processes.
Integrated feedback tools enable users to receive constructive critiques on their compositions, promoting growth and refinement in their musical skills.
Robust community support through forums and shared resources helps users troubleshoot issues and learn from each other, enhancing the overall user experience.

enriched_points.point_title: Case Studies of Successful User Experiences
enriched_points.expanded_content: Real-world examples illustrate how users have effectively utilized music AI tools, showcasing increased productivity and enhanced creativity in their compositions.
